elements.label: Storyteller
elements.description: The GM / Storyteller using the desktop app. Configures roll settings, triggers rolls, manages dyscrasias and chronicle data, and monitors the coterie via the Campaign viewer.

elements.label: Player
elements.description: A player whose character lives in a connected VTT. Surfaces in vtmtools only as the `controlled_by` field on a CanonicalCharacter — the app does not authenticate or directly serve players. Their actions in the VTT (taking damage, gaining hunger) propagate through the bridge.

elements.label: Roll20 Game Session
elements.description: The Roll20 browser-based virtual tabletop where the actual VTM game is played when running on Roll20. Source of all live Roll20 character data. The Roll20 extension runs inside this page.

elements.label: Roll20 Extension
elements.description: Chrome extension injected into Roll20 pages. Reads character data from Roll20's Backbone/Firebase data layer, debounces per-attribute Firebase events into 200ms whole-character snapshots, and relays them to the desktop app over `ws://127.0.0.1:7423`. Also receives writeback commands.

elements.label: Foundry World
elements.description: A Foundry VTT world running with the WoD5e (vtm5e) system installed. Source of all live Foundry actor data. Can be hosted anywhere (Forge, Molten, self-hosted); only the GM's browser session connects to vtmtools.

elements.label: Foundry Bridge Module
elements.description: The `vtmtools-bridge` Foundry module installed in the GM's world. Initialization is gated on `game.user.isGM` — only the GM's browser ever connects. Hooks `updateActor` / `createActor` / `deleteActor`; pushes the full actor list on `ready` then forwards individual changes. Receives outbound `actor.*` / `bridge.*` / `game.*` / `storyteller.*` wire messages and dispatches via handler-map (`scripts/bridge.js` + `scripts/foundry-actions/*.js`). Currently at module version 0.3.0 (Phase 2 game.* helpers shipped).

elements.label: WoD5e API
elements.path: (WOD5E.api.* — provided by the WoD5e Foundry system)
elements.description: The Wod5e (vtm5e) system's roll API exposed inside Foundry. Foundry-side dice mechanics for V5 — hunger dice, messy criticals, rouse checks, bestial fails — all delegated here via `WOD5E.api.RollFromDataset` (full pools) and direct `WOD5E.api.Roll` (empty-pool / rouse-check). vtmtools' game.* umbrella is a thin shim; WoD5e owns the actual mechanics.

elements.label: +layout.svelte
elements.description: Root SvelteKit layout. Owns the dark-only `:global(:root)` CSS token block (text/surface/border/accent/temperament) and the sidebar-plus-content frame. Lazy-loads the active tool from src/tools.ts. All tools render inside this shell.

elements.label: +page.svelte
elements.description: The single page. Reads the selected tool id (sidebar state), looks up the entry in src/tools.ts, awaits the dynamic import, mounts the component.

elements.label: Resonance.svelte
elements.description: The main resonance rolling tool. Holds GM Roll Config in component state, drives the Dice Engine via `roll_resonance`, displays results, and distributes them to exports, bridge writeback, and the Tool Event Bus. Also queries the Dyscrasia Store on Acute results. Reads the merged bridge live feed (via the bridge store) for character selection — works against either source.

elements.label: DyscrasiaManager.svelte
elements.description: Full CRUD interface for browsing, searching, filtering, adding, editing, and deleting dyscrasias. Also supports rolling a random dyscrasia by resonance type.

elements.label: Campaign.svelte
elements.description: Live VTT character stat display. Shows hunger, health, willpower, humanity, blood potency for all bridge-connected characters in real time across both Roll20 and Foundry. Also hosts the Saved Characters surface — Save/Update/Delete actions persist canonical snapshots, and a built-in Compare modal diffs saved-vs-live (drift badge per card; per-path before/after plus specialty-list comparison in modal).

elements.label: DomainsManager.svelte
elements.description: Hierarchical chronicle tracker. Lets the Storyteller model a game's territories, institutions, characters, and relationships as a graph of typed nodes connected by typed edges. Backed by the chronicles/nodes/edges SQLite tables; freeform node and edge type strings (ADR 0003).

elements.label: AdvantagesManager.svelte
elements.description: Manager for V5 advantages (merits/flaws/disciplines/loresheets etc.). CRUD over the advantages SQLite table via lib/advantages/api.ts.

elements.label: FoundryTest.svelte
elements.description: Dev/QA surface for the Foundry `game.*` helpers (FHL Phase 2). Lets the operator pick a Foundry actor from the bridge live feed and exercise `game.roll_v5_pool` and `game.post_chat_as_actor` end-to-end. Not a player-facing tool; exists to smoke-test the helper umbrella while downstream consumers (#9 roll-source toggle, #11 hunger/remorse/contested) are still planned.

elements.label: GmScreen.svelte
elements.description: Active-table GM surface. Composes V5 skill checks + status modifiers + character context to dispatch rolls (locally via shared/v5/* or remotely via the Foundry game.* umbrella, depending on roll-source toggle). Reads modifier and status-template stores; writes resonance/effects via bridge writeback.

elements.label: bridge.svelte.ts
elements.description: Typed bridge store. Wraps `listen('bridge://characters-updated', …)` and per-source connect/disconnect events. Components import from here; per CLAUDE.md / ARCHITECTURE.md §5, components never call `listen()` or `invoke()` directly.

elements.label: domains.svelte.ts
elements.description: Chronicle/node/edge UI state. Holds active-chronicle id, breadcrumb stack, expansion state. Backed by Tauri commands through lib/domains/api.ts.

elements.label: modifiers.svelte.ts
elements.description: Active-roll modifier stack for the GM Screen. Holds the temporary list of bonuses/penalties contributing to the next dispatched skill check pool.

elements.label: savedCharacters.svelte.ts
elements.description: Cached SavedCharacter list for the Campaign tool's Saved surface. Backed by lib/saved-characters/api.ts.

elements.label: statusTemplates.svelte.ts
elements.description: Cached status-template definitions used by the GM Screen modifier-stack composer. Backed by db/status_template.rs via lib/modifiers/api.ts.

elements.label: lib/advantages/api.ts
elements.description: Typed wrapper for advantage IPC. One exported function per Tauri command in db/advantage.rs.

elements.label: lib/bridge/api.ts
elements.description: Typed wrapper for bridge IPC: bridge_get_characters, bridge_get_status, bridge_refresh, bridge_set_attribute. Generic across Roll20 and Foundry.

elements.label: lib/character/api.ts
elements.description: Typed wrapper for character-tooling commands in tools/character.rs. Includes character-context derivation used by the GM Screen and saved-characters surfaces.

elements.label: lib/domains/api.ts
elements.description: Typed wrapper for chronicle/node/edge CRUD + tree queries. The reference implementation cited by ARCHITECTURE.md §4 — new tools follow this shape.

elements.label: lib/foundry-chat/api.ts
elements.description: Typed wrapper for the Foundry game.* umbrella: trigger_foundry_roll (game.roll_v5_pool) and post_foundry_chat (game.post_chat_as_actor). Foundry-only — Roll20 has no equivalent.

elements.label: lib/modifiers/api.ts
elements.description: Typed wrapper for modifier + status-template IPC (db/modifier.rs and db/status_template.rs).

elements.label: lib/saved-characters/api.ts
elements.description: Typed wrapper for saved-character CRUD (db/saved_character.rs). The payload type is BridgeCharacter (snake_case at the IPC boundary; see memory note on naming asymmetry).

elements.label: lib/v5/api.ts
elements.description: Typed wrapper for `roll_skill_check` (tools/skill_check.rs → shared/v5/*). Currently consumed by the GM Screen for local-roll dispatch; the V5 helper library was shipped ahead of full UI consumption per the character-tooling roadmap.

elements.label: db/chronicle.rs
elements.description: Tauri commands: list_chronicles, get_chronicle, create_chronicle, update_chronicle, delete_chronicle. Reads/writes the chronicles table; ON DELETE CASCADE rolls into nodes and edges.

elements.label: db/node.rs
elements.description: Tauri commands: list_nodes, get_node, create_node, update_node, delete_node, get_parent_of, get_children_of, get_siblings_of, get_path_to_root, get_subtree. The tree queries walk only the 'contains' edge type.

elements.label: db/edge.rs
elements.description: Tauri commands: list_edges, list_edges_for_node, create_edge, update_edge, delete_edge. UNIQUE (from, to, edge_type) plus partial-unique 'contains' parent enforcement.

elements.label: db/dyscrasia.rs
elements.description: Tauri commands: list_dyscrasias, add_dyscrasia, update_dyscrasia, delete_dyscrasia, roll_random_dyscrasia. Selects randomly from built-in + custom rows of the matching resonance type.

elements.label: db/advantage.rs
elements.description: Tauri commands for advantage CRUD. Backs the AdvantagesManager tool.

elements.label: db/modifier.rs
elements.description: Tauri commands for modifier CRUD (active-roll bonuses/penalties). Used by the GM Screen modifier stack.

elements.label: db/saved_character.rs
elements.description: Tauri commands: save/list/update/delete on the saved_characters table. Payload column carries the canonical BridgeCharacter as JSON; the Compare flow diffs it against the live merged Bridge cache.

elements.label: db/status_template.rs
elements.description: Tauri commands for status-template CRUD. Templates are reusable named modifier presets the GM Screen composes into a roll.

elements.label: tools/resonance.rs
elements.description: Tauri command: roll_resonance(RollConfig) -> ResonanceRollResult. Orchestrates the temperament pool, weighted resonance pick, acute check via shared/dice.rs and shared/resonance.rs.

elements.label: tools/export.rs
elements.description: Tauri command: export_result_to_md. Writes a single timestamped Markdown file to ~/Documents/vtmtools/ via tokio::fs (never std::fs per ARCHITECTURE.md §5).

elements.label: tools/character.rs
elements.description: Backend character-tooling commands. Derives character context (effective pools, splat-aware fields) used by the GM Screen and saved-characters surfaces.

elements.label: tools/foundry_chat.rs
elements.description: Tauri commands: trigger_foundry_roll, post_foundry_chat. Validates inputs, builds game.roll_v5_pool / game.post_chat_as_actor wire envelopes, hands them to the Foundry source for transport.

elements.label: tools/gm_screen.rs
elements.description: Backend support for the GM Screen tool — pool composition (advantages + modifiers + status templates + character context) and roll-dispatch orchestration. Routes to shared/v5/* for local rolls or the Foundry game.* umbrella for remote rolls (future #9 toggle).

elements.label: tools/skill_check.rs
elements.description: Tauri command: roll_skill_check. Thin Tauri shim over shared/v5/* (build_pool / roll_pool / interpret / format_skill_check).

elements.label: bridge/commands.rs
elements.description: Tauri commands: bridge_get_characters, bridge_get_status, bridge_refresh, bridge_set_attribute. set_attribute's `name` is opaque to the frontend and translated per-source by each BridgeSource impl.

elements.label: bridge/mod.rs (Bridge Core)
elements.description: Bridge core: accept loops on both ports, the Arc<BridgeState> (per-source ConnectionInfo + merged HashMap<String, CanonicalCharacter>), the BridgeSource trait, the rcgen-driven self-signed TLS init for wss://7424, and the canonical CanonicalCharacter type. ARCHITECTURE.md §3 / §5 / ADR 0006.

elements.label: bridge/roll20/* (Roll20 source adapter)
elements.description: BridgeSource impl for Roll20. Decodes InboundMsg::Characters | CharacterUpdate, translates into CanonicalCharacter via translate.rs (field-picking per docs/reference/roll20-bundle.md), builds outbound set_attribute / refresh JSON frames. Cache key 'roll20:<id>'.

elements.label: bridge/foundry/* (Foundry source adapter)
elements.description: BridgeSource impl for Foundry. Decodes FoundryInbound::Hello | Actors | ActorUpdate, translates the actor.system blob (vampire/werewolf/hunter splat fields side-by-side per docs/reference/foundry-vtm5e-paths.md) into CanonicalCharacter. Cache key 'foundry:<id>'. Outbound frames built by the action umbrellas.

elements.label: actor.* umbrella
elements.description: Per-actor sheet edits. Verbs: update_field, create_feature, create_item_simple, append_private_notes_line, delete_items_by_prefix, delete_item_by_id, apply_dyscrasia. Rust builders ↔ JS executors. The JS side calls actor.update / createEmbeddedDocuments on Foundry.

elements.label: bridge.* umbrella
elements.description: Connection lifecycle and metadata: extended Hello (protocol_version, capabilities, world_id), bridge.subscribe / bridge.unsubscribe, structured error envelope (Plan 0 consolidation). Not a domain umbrella — owns the wire-protocol negotiation surface.

elements.label: game.* umbrella
elements.description: Foundry-only roll + chat helpers (FHL Phase 2). game.roll_v5_pool delegates to WOD5E.api.RollFromDataset; game.post_chat_as_actor uses ChatMessage.create + getSpeaker. Hunger / messy / rouse / bestial mechanics are owned by WoD5e — vtmtools is a thin shim.

elements.label: storyteller.* umbrella
elements.description: Storyteller-scope helpers (cross-actor, scene-level operations). Reserved umbrella per the Foundry helper library roadmap — exact verbs evolve as the Storyteller-side features land.

elements.label: lib.rs (IPC handler registry + boot)
elements.description: Tauri Builder setup. Registers every command via `invoke_handler(tauri::generate_handler![...])`, builds the SqliteConnectOptions pool with PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON, runs migrations, calls db::seed (ADR 0002), and inserts BridgeSource impls into the Arc<BridgeState> sources map before start_servers. The single seam where the entire backend wires together — ARCHITECTURE.md §9.

elements.label: shared/dice.rs + shared/resonance.rs (Resonance Engine)
elements.description: Pure-Rust resonance dice library: d10 pool with take_high/take_low, threshold-based temperament tier (Negligible/Fleeting/Intense), weighted resonance type pick (SliderLevel multipliers), acute check (9–10 on a single d10). Distinct from shared/v5/* (the V5 skill-check engine). All RNG runs server-side.

elements.label: shared/v5/* (V5 Skill-Check Engine)
elements.path: src-tauri/src/shared/v5/{pool.rs, dice.rs, interpret.rs, difficulty.rs, message.rs, skill_check.rs, types.rs}
elements.description: Pure-Rust V5 skill-check library: build_pool (attribute + skill + modifiers), roll_pool (regular dice + hunger dice), interpret (successes / messy crit / bestial fail), compare (vs difficulty), format_skill_check (chat-line message). The GM Screen consumes it via roll_skill_check; broader UI consumption lands with #9 (roll-source toggle) and #11 (hunger/remorse/contested). Pre-built per the character-tooling roadmap.

elements.label: db/seed.rs (Destructive reseed)
elements.description: Deletes all is_custom=0 dyscrasia rows on every startup and reinserts the canonical V5 set. ADR 0002 — the destructive logic must NOT be reverted to a count-check guard. Custom (is_custom=1) entries are preserved across reseeds.

elements.label: DB Migrations (sqlx::migrate!)
elements.description: Forward-only schema migrations applied via sqlx::migrate! at startup. Five files at present: initial dyscrasias schema → chronicle/nodes/edges graph → advantages → saved_characters → modifiers + status_templates.

elements.label: Resonance Roll Result
elements.description: The complete outcome of a single resonance roll. Carries all temperament dice, the selected die, temperament tier, resonance type, acute check, and an optional attached dyscrasia. The product of tools/resonance.rs that flows outward to UI display, Markdown export, the bridge writeback path, and the Tool Event Bus.

elements.label: GM Roll Config (component state)
elements.description: The settings the Storyteller configures before making a resonance roll: TemperamentConfig (dice_count, take_highest, negligible_max, fleeting_max) plus ResonanceWeights (per-type SliderLevel). Lives in component state — resets on app restart, not persisted to SQLite.

elements.label: Roll History (frontend)
elements.description: In-memory list of past resonance roll results for the current session. Resets when the app restarts. Displayed in the Resonance Roller UI.

elements.label: BridgeState (canonical character cache)
elements.description: Arc<BridgeState> holding the merged HashMap<String, CanonicalCharacter> across all sources (key '<source>:<source_id>'), per-source ConnectionInfo (connected flag + outbound mpsc::Sender), and the registered BridgeSource impls. Shared between every accept loop and every Tauri command handler. Source-of-truth for everything the bridge://characters-updated event carries. ADR 0006.

elements.label: dyscrasias (SQLite table)
elements.description: Holds dyscrasia entries. is_custom=0 rows are destructively reseeded on every startup (ADR 0002); is_custom=1 rows persist. resonance_type CHECK constraint enforces the spelling 'Melancholy' (not 'Melancholic') — see ARCHITECTURE.md §6 invariants.

elements.label: chronicles + nodes + edges (Chronicle Store)
elements.description: Three-table chronicle graph. node.type and edge.edge_type are freeform user-authored strings (ADR 0003). ON DELETE CASCADE rolls chronicle deletions through nodes through edges. Partial unique index forbids more than one 'contains' parent per node — the contains sub-graph is a strict tree.

elements.label: advantages (SQLite table)
elements.description: Holds V5 advantage definitions (merits/flaws/disciplines/loresheets). Backs the AdvantagesManager tool.

elements.label: saved_characters (SQLite table)
elements.description: Canonical-payload snapshots of bridge-imported characters. Each row: source attribution (Roll20/Foundry), source id, BridgeCharacter payload (snake_case at IPC boundary), timestamps. Persisted independently of live VTT state — survives bridge disconnect. Compare modal in Campaign diffs saved-vs-live.

elements.label: modifiers + status_templates (SQLite tables)
elements.description: Two tables for the GM Screen's modifier-stack composer. modifiers: ad-hoc bonuses/penalties; status_templates: reusable named presets the GM can apply repeatedly (e.g. 'concealed', 'wounded', 'using a discipline').

elements.label: Markdown export dir (~/Documents/vtmtools/)
elements.description: One timestamped Markdown file per export, written by tools/export.rs via tokio::fs. Each file contains the full ResonanceRollResult, attached dyscrasia (if any), and an export timestamp. One of two write paths in the security scope (ARCHITECTURE.md §8).

elements.label: ws://127.0.0.1:7423 (Roll20 wire)
elements.description: Plain WebSocket port. Connects the Tauri app to the Roll20 Chrome extension. Preserves the pre-bridge wire protocol byte-for-byte so the existing extension keeps working. Bidirectional: inbound Characters/CharacterUpdate, outbound set_attribute/refresh.

elements.label: wss://127.0.0.1:7424 (Foundry wire)
elements.description: TLS WebSocket port. Connects the Tauri app to the Foundry vtmtools-bridge module. wss is non-optional because Foundry is commonly served over HTTPS and browsers block ws://localhost from HTTPS pages as mixed content. Self-signed cert generated by rcgen on first launch and persisted in the app data dir; GM accepts the cert warning once per browser. Carries dot-namespaced wire frames (actor.* / bridge.* / game.* / storyteller.*).

elements.label: bridge://characters-updated
elements.description: Backend-to-frontend event carrying the full merged Vec<CanonicalCharacter> across all sources. Frontend re-renders from the full list, never from a diff. Fired any time any source pushes an update.

elements.label: bridge://*/connected · disconnected (lifecycle)
elements.description: Per-source bridge connection lifecycle events: bridge://roll20/connected, bridge://roll20/disconnected, bridge://foundry/connected, bridge://foundry/disconnected. Frontend toggles the per-source connection pip in the sidebar. Disconnect is expected flow, not error.

elements.label: Tool Event Bus
elements.description: Svelte writable store carrying frontend-to-frontend events for cross-tool coordination without direct coupling. Currently emits resonance_result (temperament + resonance type + acute + dyscrasia name); no subscribers yet — kept as the seam for future cross-tool features. Distinct from the Tauri Event Bridge (backend-to-frontend).

connections.label: configures + clicks Roll
connections.description: GM dials TemperamentConfig + ResonanceWeights and triggers a roll.

connections.label: remote roll via game.roll_v5_pool (#9 toggle, future)
rolls into Foundry chat; sheet-side mutations
